mitre_attack: T1590.001 - Gather Victim Network Information: Domain Properties
T1592.004 - Gather Victim Host Information: Client Configurations
cwe: CWE-200 - Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

threat.title: GraphQL Introspection Enabled in Production
threat.description: GraphQL introspection queries allow attackers to enumerate the entire API schema, discovering all types, fields, mutations, queries, and arguments. This enables attackers to map out the complete attack surface, identify sensitive operations, discover deprecated or internal fields, and craft targeted exploitation attempts. In production environments, introspection should be disabled to prevent unauthorized schema discovery.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker discovers GraphQL endpoint at /graphql or /api/graphql through reconnaissance
2. Sends introspection query (__schema, __type) without authentication to enumerate complete schema
3. Discovers sensitive types like 'AdminUser', 'InternalConfig', or 'PrivateDocument' in schema response
4. Identifies hidden fields such as 'ssn', 'creditCard', 'internalNotes' not exposed in public documentation
5. Crafts targeted queries to access sensitive data or discovers deprecated mutations still accessible
6. Exploits discovered fields to extract confidential information or execute unauthorized operations
prerequisites: GraphQL introspection queries enabled in production
GraphQL endpoint accessible without authentication or with weak authentication
Schema contains sensitive types, fields, or operations
No query filtering or allowlisting implemented

cve_references.description: Information Disclosure in OpenShift Console via GraphQL introspection allowing attackers to discover API structure and craft targeted attacks
Spring for GraphQL batch loader function exposed security context from different session

real_world_breaches.impact: Complete schema enumeration revealing all endpoints and capabilities of promotion banner management system
real_world_breaches.root_cause: GraphQL endpoint from third-party integration had introspection enabled, allowing full enumeration

real_world_breaches.incident: GraphQL API Attack Study - Imperva Research
real_world_breaches.impact: 50% of GraphQL endpoints targeted with introspection attacks; majority used __schema field for API discovery
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Default configurations leaving introspection enabled in production environments

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Introspection query leaked sensitive GraphQL system information including internal types and fields
GraphQL introspection enabled allowing attackers to fetch schemas, types, fields, and available operations without authentication
GraphQL introspection query executable through unauthenticated WebSocket connection, revealing complete API schema

security_research.title: GraphQL Cheat Sheet - Introspection Security
security_research.key_finding: OWASP recommends disabling introspection system-wide in production. For internal APIs, disable entirely; for public APIs, restrict to authenticated users only

security_research.title: Testing GraphQL - OWASP Web Security Testing Guide v4.2
security_research.key_finding: Introspection should be disabled on production environments as it allows data extraction and gathering. Access control alone is insufficient protection

security_research.title: Graph Security - Apollo GraphQL Technical Notes
security_research.key_finding: Introspection is the fastest way for bad actors to learn about schema. Disable in production and limit access to staging environments where enabled

mitigations.title: Disable Introspection in Production (Apollo Server 4.x)
mitigations.description: Completely disable GraphQL introspection queries in production environments by setting introspection: false in Apollo Server configuration. This prevents attackers from discovering the schema structure while maintaining introspection in development for developer tooling.
iso27001_controls: A.14.1.2 - Securing application services on public networks
nist_csf: PR.PT-3 - Access to systems is controlled
validation_steps: Set introspection: false in Apollo Server config for production
Deploy to production environment
Attempt introspection query: { __schema { queryType { name } } }
Verify query returns error: 'GraphQL introspection is not allowed'
Confirm development environment still has introspection enabled
Test that legitimate queries still function correctly

mitigations.title: Implement Query Allowlisting (Persisted Queries)
mitigations.description: Use persisted queries to create an allowlist of approved GraphQL operations. Only pre-registered query hashes are executed, preventing arbitrary queries including introspection attempts. This approach provides defense-in-depth even if introspection blocking fails.

code_example.framework: Apollo Server 4.x with Automatic Persisted Queries

iso27001_controls: A.14.1.2 - Securing application services on public networks
nist_csf: PR.PT-3 - Access to systems and assets is controlled
validation_steps: Install @apollo/client/link/persisted-queries package
Generate and register allowed query hashes
Deploy server with persisted query enforcement
Test legitimate queries with valid hashes - should succeed
Attempt arbitrary query without hash - should fail
Attempt introspection query - should fail
Monitor logs for rejected query attempts

mitigations.title: Require Authentication for Introspection Queries
mitigations.description: Implement custom validation rules to allow introspection only for authenticated admin users. This provides granular control for internal tools while blocking public access.
nist_csf: PR.AC-1 - Identities and credentials are managed
validation_steps: Implement custom validation rule for introspection fields
Configure JWT authentication middleware
Test introspection without auth - should fail with 403
Test introspection with regular user token - should fail
Test introspection with admin token - should succeed
Verify error messages don't leak sensitive information
Monitor authentication failures in SIEM

indicators: HTTP POST requests to /graphql endpoint containing '__schema' or '__type' keywords
Large response payloads (>50KB) from GraphQL endpoint indicating schema dump
Sequential queries testing different field names (brute force enumeration)
GraphQL queries with unusually deep nesting or field selection
Requests to GraphQL endpoint from reconnaissance tools (Burp Suite, Postman)
Multiple 403/error responses followed by successful schema queries
GraphQL queries from unauthenticated or low-privilege users

log_queries.query:
// Detect GraphQL introspection attempts
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where CsMethod == "POST" and CsUriStem contains "/graphql"
| extend RequestBody = parse_json(CsRequestBody)
| where RequestBody.query contains "__schema" or RequestBody.query contains "__type"
| extend IntrospectionType = case(
    RequestBody.query contains "__schema", "Full Schema Query",
    RequestBody.query contains "__type", "Type Introspection",
    "Unknown"
  )
| summarize 
    AttemptCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(CIp),
    StatusCodes = make_set(ScStatus),
    AvgResponseSize = avg(ScBytes)
  by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m), CIp, CsUserAgent, IntrospectionType
| where AttemptCount > 3 or AvgResponseSize > 50000
| project TimeGenerated, CIp, CsUserAgent, IntrospectionType, AttemptCount, AvgResponseSize, StatusCodes
| order by TimeGenerated desc

// GraphQL introspection query detection
event.category: "web" AND http.request.method: "POST" 
AND url.path: "/graphql" 
AND (http.request.body.content: "*__schema*" OR http.request.body.content: "*__type*")
| stats count() by source.ip, user_agent.original, http.response.status_code, bin(@timestamp, 5m)
| where count > 3 OR http.response.bytes > 50000

index=web_logs sourcetype=graphql_access
| rex field=request_body "(?<introspection_field>__schema|__type)"
| where isnotnull(introspection_field)
| eval response_size_kb=response_bytes/1024
| stats 
    count as attempt_count,
    dc(client_ip) as unique_ips,
    avg(response_size_kb) as avg_response_kb,
    values(status_code) as status_codes,
    values(introspection_field) as fields_queried
  by _time, client_ip, user_agent
| where attempt_count > 3 OR avg_response_kb > 50
| eval severity=case(
    avg_response_kb > 100, "Critical",
    avg_response_kb > 50, "High",
    attempt_count > 10, "High",
    1=1, "Medium"
  )
| table _time, client_ip, user_agent, attempt_count, avg_response_kb, fields_queried, status_codes, severity
| sort -_time

# GraphQL introspection monitoring for API Gateway/ALB
fields @timestamp, @message, requestParameters.sourceIPAddress as clientIP
| filter @message like /\"__schema\"/ or @message like /\"__type\"/
| parse @message /"query":"(?<graphql_query>.*?)"/
| stats count(*) as requests, 
        sum(responseElements.contentLength) as total_bytes,
        avg(responseElements.contentLength) as avg_response_size
  by bin(5m) as time_window, clientIP
| filter requests > 3 or avg_response_size > 51200
| sort @timestamp desc

tools.name: GraphQL Voyager
tools.usage: Point at GraphQL endpoint to visualize schema - should fail if introspection disabled

tools.name: InQL Scanner (Burp Suite)
tools.usage: Automated introspection testing and vulnerability scanning for GraphQL endpoints
